title: Make Codex build ChefFlow continuously
rawAskSummary: David wants Codex to build the project in the background 24/7 while he is sleeping, away, or working elsewhere.
statusReason: The governed V1 builder runtime scaffold now exists with queue, claim, next-task, receipt, and focused test commands. A resident scheduler is the next explicit runtime step.

title: Wire live background Codex execution
rawAskSummary: David approved the next step to make the V1 builder actually invoke Codex in the background instead of only watching in dry-run mode.
statusReason: The live runner now creates isolated worktrees, invokes Codex exec for approved V1 queue tasks, requires a pushed task branch, updates claims, and records receipts.

title: Spec: Configurable Overhead % and Labor Rate in Plate Cost
rawAskSummary: The true plate cost calculation uses hardcoded `DEFAULT_OVERHEAD_PERCENT = 15` and `DEFAULT_HOURLY_RATE_CENTS = 5000` ($50/hr). These should come from the chef's pricing config instead. This spec adds two columns to `chef_pricing_config`, two fields to the settings form, and threads the values through the plate cost calculator.
